key_points: Digestion is a multi-step process: Ingestion, Digestion, Absorption, Assimilation, and Egestion.
The human heart has four chambers (two atria and two ventricles) to prevent the mixing of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.
Capillaries are thin-walled blood vessels where the actual exchange of gases and nutrients takes place.
Anaerobic respiration occurs in our muscle cells during heavy exercise, producing lactic acid and causing cramps.
The excretory system in humans consists of two kidneys, two ureters, a urinary bladder, and a urethra.
examples: Amoeba Digestion: Uses pseudopodia to engulf food particles into a food vacuole.
Ruminants: Animals like cows have a four-chambered stomach to digest cellulose via rumination.
Excretory Products: Ammonia (fish), Urea (humans), and Uric Acid (birds/lizards).
Pulse: The rhythmic throbbing of arteries caused by the pumping of the heart, felt at the wrist.
formulas: Anaerobic Respiration (Yeast): $Glucose \rightarrow Alcohol + Carbon \ dioxide + Energy$
Pulse Rate = Number of heartbeats per minute
exam_tips: Differentiate between 'Arteries' (carry oxygen-rich blood away from heart) and 'Veins' (carry carbon dioxide-rich blood to heart).
Remember that the Pulmonary Artery is the only artery that carries deoxygenated blood.
Note that Villi in the small intestine increase surface area for faster absorption of digested food.
Understand that Dialysis is an artificial way to filter blood when kidneys fail.
